prompt: tutor me on how the parse pipeline works in this repo, end to end. I want to actually understand it deeply (deep enough to debug a stuck job in prod), not just memorize file names. let's start by agreeing on a learning plan, then run lesson 1.

assertions.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions about goal/prior knowledge/scope) BEFORE proposing the curriculum.
assertions.check: Look for explicit clarifying questions early in the response, asked before the lesson list is produced.

assertions.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved (copied into outputs/curriculum.md).
Curriculum contains a clear single-paragraph Goal section.
assertions.check: Look for a '## Goal' heading or equivalent with a concrete success criterion (not just 'understand the pipeline').

assertions.text: Curriculum has between 3 and 7 lessons (the sweet spot the skill prescribes).
assertions.check: Count top-level lesson bullets in the Lessons section.

assertions.text: Curriculum or lesson 1 references at least 3 real file paths from this repo (backend/, llamaparse/, jobs/, etc.).
assertions.check: Look for clickable markdown links to files like backend/app/..., llamaparse/..., or backend/jobs/... Verify the referenced files exist if possible.

assertions.text: Lesson 1 opens with an explicit, verifiable goal statement (one sentence).
assertions.check: Look for 'by the end of this lesson' or equivalent at the start of lesson 1.

assertions.text: Lesson 1 grounds in specific repo files first, then generalizes to the underlying concept (concrete → concept order).
assertions.check: Verify the structural sequence: real-file references appear before the conceptual explanation, not after.

assertions.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active comprehension check (predict-then-verify, sketch, debug-this scenario, find-an-example).
assertions.check: Look for an explicit exercise at the end — not a trivia quiz.
